title: ‘Over a foot of manure’: Horses, mules among animals rescued from Western Mass. property, MSPCA says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MSPCA-Angell rescued 13 animals, including horses and a mule, from a Massachusetts farm with poor conditions.
Some animals were found standing in over a foot of manure and had overgrown hooves, making walking difficult.
The rescued animals are being treated at MSPCA-Angell at Nevins Farm and are available for adoption.

title: 10 players Bruins could target in 1st round of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins hold the No. 23 overall pick in the 2026 NHL Draft, among eight total selections.
Potential targets for the Bruins include Tommy Bleyl, a high-scoring defenseman from the QMJHL.
The Bruins could also consider forwards Nikita Klepov and J.P. Hurlbert for offensive skills.

title: Coyote warning issued in Beverly after dog attacked on trail
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Beverly police issued a warning after a dog was attacked by a coyote at the Fox Hill Trail entrance.
Officers found a coyote and her pups in a den 30 feet from the trailhead, leaving them undisturbed.
Residents are advised to leash dogs, avoid coyote dens, and report aggressive behavior during denning season.

title: Preserving a vehicle’s computer settings while replacing a battery
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul from AAA Northeast advises against running a car's engine while changing the battery to preserve computer settings.
Advanced battery chargers or portable jump starters can maintain vehicle memory during battery replacement by providing constant voltage.
Avoiding hard acceleration for a few minutes after starting a car allows vital fluids to circulate, reducing engine wear.

title: Nationwide rental listings are most affordable in years, Zillow says. In Boston? Not quite.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Zillow reports 74% of national rental listings are affordable, but only 50.2% are affordable in Boston.
Boston's typical rent is $3,211, the fourth-highest in the U.S., with only 0.4% of listings under $1,000.
Boston's rent increased by 2.5% annually, while a rent control proposal was blocked by Massachusetts' highest court.

title: Health officials: State’s first West Nile virus-positive mosquito of the year found in Berkshires
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Massachusetts detected its first West Nile virus-positive mosquitoes of 2026 in a Clarksburg lab.
No humans or animals have been diagnosed with the virus in the state as of June 25.
Health officials recommend insect repellent and removing standing water to prevent mosquito breeding.

title: Hooters closes its last locations in Massachusetts
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Hooters closed its last three Massachusetts locations in Dedham, Saugus, and West Springfield, ending its presence in the state.
The closures were made by Hooters of New York/New England, a franchise group that has operated in the region for over 30 years.
These Massachusetts closures are unrelated to the Hooters of America Chapter 11 bankruptcy filed in 2025.

title: Former Middleborough fire captain charged for allegedly secretly filming nude stepdaughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eric Gunnison, 59, faces 23 charges related to secretly filming his nude stepdaughter without consent, plus other charges.
Gunnison, former Middleborough fire captain, was placed on leave in November 2025 and later resigned amid accusations.
Gunnison pleaded not guilty and was released on conditions, with a court hearing scheduled for August 20.

title: How to watch the Parade of Sail by boat
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 250's Parade of Sail features over 60 ships from 20 countries, beginning July 11 in Boston Harbor.
Charter operators offer experiences like a $318 excursion on the Cutter Bloodhound from Provincetown, including lunch and snacks.
City Cruises provides an eight-hour Parade of Sail tour with narration and viewing decks, departing from 1 Long Wharf for $149.

title: Officials identify cyclist, 15, killed in Franklin crash as Bishop Feehan student
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, a Bishop Feehan High School student, died after being hit by a vehicle in Franklin.
The accident occurred near King Street and I-495 north off-ramp; police received multiple 911 calls around 3:20 p.m.
The driver remained on scene and is cooperating with police; the investigation into the crash is ongoing.
